CGAL::Color.name: CGAL::Color
CGAL::Color.brief: An object of the class Color is a color available for drawing operations in many CGAL output streams.
CGAL::Color.detailed: Each color is defined by a 4 unsigned chars (r,g,b,a) with 0 $\le$ r,g,b,a $ \le $ 255, the so-called rgba-value of the color. The alpha parameter (representing transparency) is often ignored and left to its default value (255 = no transparency), which is why we often refer to the rgb-value of the color.

members.name: Color
members.brief: creates a color with rgba-value (0,0,0,255) , i.e. black.

members.name: Color
members.brief: creates a color with rgba-value ( red , green , blue , alpha ) .

CGAL::Input_rep.name: CGAL::Input_rep
CGAL::Input_rep.brief: The definition of Input_rep is completely symmetric to Output_rep .

members.name: Input_rep
members.brief: initialize with a reference to t .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: perform the input, calls operator>> by default.

members.name: operator>>
members.brief: stream input to the Input_rep calls its operator() .
members.detailed: CGAL defines input operators for classes that are derived from the class std::istream . This allows to read from input streams as std::cin , as well as from std::istringstream and std::ifstream . The input operator is defined for all classes in the CGAL Kernel .

CGAL::IO::Basic_color_stream_guard.name: CGAL::IO::Basic_color_stream_guard
CGAL::IO::Basic_color_stream_guard.brief: RAII helper class for temporarily installing a color streambuf on a stream.
CGAL::IO::Basic_color_stream_guard.detailed: This class automatically restores the original streambuf when it goes out of scope. It provides a convenient way to add colors to output streams within a specific scope. StreamT The stream type (e.g., std::ostream , std::wostream ) Basic_color_streambuf make_color_guards

members.name: Basic_color_stream_guard
members.brief: constructs and installs a color streambuf with one or more colors.
members.detailed: AnsiColors Variadic list of Ansi_color values stream The stream to modify colors One or more colors to combine (e.g., Ansi_color::Bold , Ansi_color::Red )

CGAL::IO::Basic_color_streambuf.name: CGAL::IO::Basic_color_streambuf
CGAL::IO::Basic_color_streambuf.brief: The class template Basic_color_streambuf wraps another basic_streambuf and automatically adds ANSI color codes to the output.
CGAL::IO::Basic_color_streambuf.detailed: This is useful for colorizing terminal output with consistent color schemes. CharT Character type (typically char or wchar_t ) Traits Character traits type Basic_color_stream_guard make_color_guards

members.name: make_color_code
members.brief: Generate ANSI escape sequence for the set of colors.

members.name: Basic_color_streambuf
members.brief: constructs a color streambuf wrapper with one or more colors.
members.detailed: Colors are automatically disabled if the wrapped buffer is not connected to a color-capable terminal (checked via isatty() and TERM variable), or if the NO_COLOR environment variable is set and non-empty. AnsiColors Variadic list of Ansi_color values wrapped_buf The underlying streambuf to wrap colors One or more colors to combine (e.g., Ansi_color::Bold , Ansi_color::Red )

members.brief: gets the current color code string.

members.brief: checks if colors are enabled for this streambuf.

members.brief: detects if the wrapped buffer supports color output.

CGAL::IO::Basic_indenting_stream_guard.brief: RAII helper class for temporarily installing an indenting streambuf on a stream.
CGAL::IO::Basic_indenting_stream_guard.detailed: This class automatically restores the original streambuf when it goes out of scope. It provides a convenient way to add indentation to output streams within a specific scope. StreamT The stream type (e.g., std::ostream , std::wostream ) Basic_indenting_streambuf make_indenting_guards

CGAL::Istream_iterator.name: CGAL::Istream_iterator
CGAL::Istream_iterator.brief: The class Istream_iterator is an input iterator adaptor for the input stream class Stream and value type T .
CGAL::Istream_iterator.detailed: It is particularly useful for classes that are similar but not compatible to std::istream . Is model of InputIterator

members.name: Istream_iterator
members.detailed: This is a past-the-end iterator, and it is useful when constructing a range.

members.name: Istream_iterator
members.brief: creates an input iterator reading from s .
members.detailed: When s reaches end of stream, this iterator will compare equal to an end-of-stream iterator created using the default constructor.

CGAL::Ostream_iterator.name: CGAL::Ostream_iterator
CGAL::Ostream_iterator.brief: The class Ostream_iterator is an output iterator adaptor for the output stream class Stream and value type T .
CGAL::Ostream_iterator.detailed: Is model of OutputIterator Implementation The operator*() in class Ostream_iterator uses a proxy class.

members.name: Ostream_iterator
members.brief: creates an output iterator writing to s .

CGAL::Output_rep.name: CGAL::Output_rep
CGAL::Output_rep.brief: The purpose of Output_rep is to provide a way to control output formatting that works independently of the object's stream output operator.
CGAL::Output_rep.detailed: If you don't specialize Output_rep for T , T 's stream output operator is called from within Output_rep , by default. If you want another behavior for your type T , you have to provide a specialization for that type. Furthermore, you can provide specializations with a second template parameter (a formatting tag). The second template parameter defaults to Null_tag and means default behavior . Specializations of Output_rep should provide the following features: template < class F > struct Output_rep < Some_type, F > { static const bool is_specialized = true ; Output_rep ( const Some_type& t ); std::ostream& operator() ( std::ostream& os ) const ; }; You can also specialize for a formatting tag F . The constant is_specialized can be tested by meta-programming tools to verify that a given type can be used with oformat() . Its value has to be true in a specialization of Output_rep . When there is no specialization for a type, the class template Output_rep defines is_specialized to the default value false .

members.name: Output_rep
members.brief: initialize with a const reference to t .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: perform the output, calls operator<< by default.

members.name: operator<<
members.brief: stream output of the Output_rep calls its operator() .
members.detailed: CGAL defines output operators for classes that are derived from the class std::ostream . This enables to write to output streams as std::cout or std::cerr , as well as to std::ostringstream and std::ofstream . The output operator is defined for all classes in the CGAL Kernel and for the class Color as well.

members.name: operator<<

The output operator is defined for all classes in the CGAL Kernel and for the class Color as well.

The output operator is defined for all classes in the CGAL Kernel and for the class Color as well.

CGAL::Verbose_ostream.name: CGAL::Verbose_ostream
CGAL::Verbose_ostream.brief: The class Verbose_ostream can be used as an output stream.
CGAL::Verbose_ostream.detailed: The stream output operator << is defined for any type. The class Verbose_ostream stores in an internal state a stream and whether the output is active or not. If the state is active, the stream output operator << uses the internal stream to output its argument. If the state is inactive, nothing happens. Example The class Verbose_ostream can be conveniently used to implement for example the is_valid() member function for triangulations or other complex data structures. bool is_valid ( bool verbose = false , int level = 0) { Verbose_ostream verr( verbose); verr << "Triangulation::is_valid( level = " << level << ')' << endl; verr << " Number of vertices = " << size_of_vertices() << endl; // ... }

members.name: Verbose_ostream
members.brief: creates an output stream with state set to active that writes to the stream out .

members.name: operator<<
members.brief: writes the object t into the stream out .
